exercises.name: Kettlebell Kickstand Swing
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in both hands. Stand on one leg, with the other leg slightly behind for balance. 

Swing the kettlebell between your legs, then thrust your hips forward to bring it to eye level. Focuses on the hips, glutes, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Kneeling Overhead Press
exercises.description:
Kneel with one knee on the ground and the other foot in front. Hold a kettlebell in each hand at shoulder height, then press overhead. Lower and repeat. 

This targets shoulders and core while improving stability.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Lateral Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand. 

Step out to the side with one leg, bending that knee while keeping the other leg straight. 

Push off the bent leg to return to standing. This targets the quads, glutes, and inner thighs.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Lateral Step Down
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand and stand beside a bench or platform. 

Step sideways onto the bench with one foot, then bring the other foot to meet it. 

Step back down with the same foot. This targets the quads, glutes, and improves balance.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Lateral Step Up
exercises.description:
Stand next to a bench or platform, hold kettlebells in both hands. 

Step sideways onto the platform, then step down. Engages legs, glutes, and core.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Lunge to Halo
exercises.description:
Stand tall holding a kettlebell at chest level. Step forward into a forward lunge, bending both knees until the front thigh is parallel to the floor and the rear knee hovers above the ground. 

Push through the front heel to return to standing, then move the kettlebell in a controlled circular motion around your head (halo) in one direction.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Lunge to Overhead Press
exercises.alternateName: KB reverse lunge single side to shoulder press
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright holding kettlebells at shoulder height with your elbows and palms away from the body.

2.Take a step forward, dropping your back knee towards the floor, at the same time, press the kettlebells overhead, extending arms fully.

3.Push off your front foot and lower the kettlebells at shoulder level returning to starting position.

4.Alternate legs on each repetition.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Overhead Carry
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell overhead with one or both hands and walk a set distance. 

This strengthens shoulders, core, and improves balance.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Overhead Standing March
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a kettlebell with both hands overhead and arms fully extended. 

Keep your core engaged and shoulders stable. Lift one knee toward your chest in a controlled march, then lower it and lift the other knee. 

Continue alternating knees while keeping the kettlebell overhead throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Pass Around The Body
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, maintain a set abdominal and set muscle glutes in order to limit the amount of body sway. 

2) Then the other direction.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Plank Pull Through
exercises.description:
Start in a plank position with a kettlebell beside you. 

Reach under your body with one hand, pulling the kettlebell across to the opposite side. 

Alternate sides. 

This strengthens the core, shoulders, and improves stability.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Plie Squat
exercises.description:
1) Start by standing straight in a wide stance with toes angled out. 

2) Hold a dumbbell with both hands in front of you and your arms extended downwards. 

3) Start by descending your body into a squat until your thighs are almost parallel to the floor. 

4) Pause, and return to starting position by pushing against your heels.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Pop Clean
exercises.description:
Swing the kettlebell up from the ground and “pop” it into the clean position by pulling your elbow high and keeping the kettlebell close to your body. 

Engages legs, hips, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Pop Clean And Squat
exercises.description:
Perform a pop clean, then immediately squat down with the kettlebell in the clean position. 

This works the legs, hips, and core while improving explosive power.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Pop Clean To Squat
exercises.description:
Swing the kettlebell up into the clean position, then transition into a squat. 

This strengthens legs, hips, and shoulders while improving movement flow.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Pullover Deadbug
exercises.description:
Perform a deadbug movement while holding a kettlebell in both hands. 

Extend one arm overhead and the opposite leg while keeping the kettlebell close to your body. 

This targets the core and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Alternating Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position. Step forward into a lunge, then alternate legs while maintaining a stable torso. 

This engages legs, glutes, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position. Step back into a reverse lunge with one leg, then alternate legs while maintaining good posture. 

This targets the legs, glutes, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked And Overhead Carry
exercises.description:
Hold one kettlebell in the rack position and the other overhead. 

Walk for a set distance, alternating the kettlebell positions. 

This targets shoulders, arms, and core stability.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.alternateName: Kettlebell Rear Foot Elevated Split Squat Racked
exercises.description:
Place one foot on an elevated surface. Hold a kettlebell in a racked position on the same side as the elevated foot. 

Lower into a split squat, keeping the chest up and engaging the core, then press back to standing.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Carry
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position (at shoulder height) and walk with it by your side. 

This engages the shoulders, arms, and core while improving stability.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position at shoulder height. Step forward into a lunge, lowering your back knee toward the ground. Push back to standing.

This targets the legs and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Ipsilateral Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell or dumbbell in a racked position on the same side as the lunging leg. 

Step forward into a lunge, lowering the back knee, then press back to standing.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Kickstand Squat
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position. Stand with one foot forward and the other foot slightly behind you. 

Squat down while keeping your chest lifted. This targets the legs and glutes, while engaging the shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Lateral Step Up
exercises.description:
Stand on a platform with one foot. Lower your body by bending the knee of the standing leg, and step the other foot down to the ground. 

Step back up onto the platform with the same foot. This strengthens legs and improves lateral movement.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position at shoulder height. 

Step one leg back into a reverse lunge while keeping your chest upright. Return to the starting position. 

This focuses on quads, glutes, and core.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Racked Walking Lunges
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in the rack position at shoulder height. 

Perform walking lunges while maintaining a stable torso. This targets the legs, glutes, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Renegade Row
exercises.description:
1) Support your body on your toes with your hands on kettlebells and your arms straight.

2) Raise one kettlebell up to your shoulder, bending your elbow, shifting your weight to the opposite side.

3) Lower the kettlebell and repeat on the other side.

4) Alternate sides with each rep.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell in each hand at your sides. 

Step one foot back into a lunge, bending both knees. Push back to the starting position. 

This targets quads, hamstrings, and glutes.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Reverse Lunge with Contralateral Press
exercises.alternateName: single leg with single arm kettlebell lunge to shoulder press
exercises.description: Perform a reverse lunge with one leg while pressing a kettlebell overhead with the opposite arm in a racked position.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Reverse Lunge with Ipsilateral Press
exercises.alternateName: single leg with single arm kettlebell lunge to shoulder press
exercises.description: Perform a reverse lunge while pressing a kettlebell overhead with the same-side arm in a racked position.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Romanian Deadlift To Bent Over Row
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell with both hands. 

Perform a Romanian deadlift by hinging at the hips, keeping a slight bend in the knees. Once you reach the lowest point, pull the kettlebell up into a bent-over row. 

This targets hamstrings, glutes, and upper back.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Side Lunge to Press
exercises.description:
Stand tall, holding a kettlebell at chest level in the hand opposite to the leg you’ll step out. 

Step one foot out to the side into a side lunge, bending the stepping knee while keeping the opposite leg straight. 

Push through the stepping heel to return to standing, then press the kettlebell overhead.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Sidestep Swing
exercises.description:
Swing the kettlebell side to side, stepping laterally as you swing. 

This targets the hips, core, and shoulders.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Single Arm Clean
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a kettlebell in one hand between your feet, arms straight. The other hand for balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward pulling the kettlebell straight up, catching it at shoulder height, bending at the elbow. Absorb the kettlebell at your shoulder by dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Return to starting position. Keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout.

4.Repeat. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Single Arm Clean and Jerk
exercises.alternateName: SA KB Clean and Jerk press
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a kettlebell in one hand between your feet with your arm straight, the other hand for balance.

2.Thrust your hips forward pulling the kettlebell straight up, catching it at shoulder height, dipping at the hips and knees slightly.

3.Quickly press the bell overhead splitting your feet front to back to absorb it t the top.

4.Return to starting position. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Single Arm Clean and Press
exercises.alternateName: KB SA Clean and shoulder press
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you want to ensure back and shoulder safety. At the bottom of the clean, keep a slight arch to your back and drive from your legs, primarily your hips on the way up.

2.Once you're going to do the press part, lockout your arm besides your ear up overhead.

3.Place the Kettlebell between your feet. As you bend down to grab the Kettlebell, push your butt back and keep your eyes looking forward.

4.Clean the Kettlebell to your shoulders by extending through the legs and hips as you raise the Kettlebell towards your shoulder. The wrist should rotate as you do so.

5.Return the weight to the starting position.

6.When you complete one side, then do the other.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Single Arm Clean and Push Press
exercises.alternateName: KB SA clean and shoulder press
exercises.description:
1.Squat holding a kettlebell in one hand between your feet with your arms straight. The other hand for balance.

2.Squat down a few inches and reverse the motion rapidly, bringing the kettlebell at shoulder level.

3.Use the momentum from the legs to drive the kettlebell overhead.

4.Once the kettlebell is locked out, lower the kettlebell to your shoulder. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Single Arm Extended Range Floor Press
exercises.description:
1) Lie on the floor, holding a kettlebell in one hand with palm facing up, the other hand placed on the floor for stability.

2) Press the kettlebell by extending the elbow and adducting the arm, with your trap getting off the floor, pressing it above your body.

3) Lower the kettlebell down returning to starting position.

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Single Arm Floor Press
exercises.description:
1) Lie on the floor with one hand holding the kettlebell, at shoulder level, palm facing up.

2) Push the kettlebell straight up toward the ceiling.

3) Lower the kettlebell returning to starting position.

4) Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.
